title: Using Monday to Create Bids for Installation Projects
excerpt: Hi All – We are a sports equipment installation company and utilize Monday.com for all of our project management. Our current bidding software is grossly out of date and I am looking into ways we could utilize Monday.com …

posts.author: brycecathcart (Bryce Cathcart)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi All – We are a sports equipment installation company and utilize Monday.com for all of our project management. Our current bidding software is grossly out of date and I am looking into ways we could utilize Monday.com to create bids. Ideally we could load all of our product data and installer pricing into the system and put together custom bids from that. Any ideas? Thanks in advance for your input/help!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: With the CRM, you could use the new Quotes & Invoices feature. https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/21050405375762-The-new-Quotes-Invoices

posts.author: Neeljy (Neelam)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bryce Cathcart:

Hi All – We are a sports equipment installation company and utilize Monday.com for all of our project management. Our current bidding software is grossly out of date and I am looking into ways we could utilize Monday.com to create bids. Ideally we could load all of our product data and installer pricing into the system and put together custom bids from that. Any ideas? Thanks in advance for your input/help!

Hi!

You can use Monday.com for bidding by:

  1. Products Board – Store all equipment, pricing, and installer costs.
  2. Bids Board – Use connected boards & mirror columns to pull pricing into custom bids.
  3. Automations & Templates – Streamline calculations, approvals, and document generation.
  4. Dashboards – Track bid performance and costs.

For advanced needs, Monday API or integrations (DocuGen, Make, Zapier) can help. Hope this helps!

posts.author: Sam_Cnet (Sam Bazargan)
posts.cookedHtml:
The way I would approach it is with a bid board with columns for project name, documents, project cost and client.

Tie this together with some automation for email approvals you have the basics of managing your project proposals.

Feel free to reach out to me directly at admin@achieveapplabs.com.

posts.author: ScottL (Scott Levy)
posts.cookedHtml: Did you figure a good way to do this?

posts.author: brycecathcart (Bryce Cathcart)
posts.cookedHtml:
I didnt… Our company decided to implement Hubspot for our quoting and crm activities so I stopped working on the solution.

Bryce Cathcart | Project Manager
